summary: Tested Price Alerts pivot (Crypto Bag Tracker v2.2). Backend: 13/14 pytest passed (1 failure due to external CoinGecko 429 rate-limit). Frontend: all tab navigation, login, subscription banner, alert list, and Bag Tracker Beta flows verified working end-to-end with the provided test user.

minor.issue: Returns 404 'Could not fetch price for BTC' when CoinGecko free-tier rate limit (HTTP 429) is hit. Direct curl to api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price confirmed 429 'You've exceeded the Rate Limit'. The endpoint has no fallback provider and no cached-fallback on 429. Alert creation still succeeds but stores current_price=0.
Same CoinGecko dependency - listing alerts calls get_price per alert; under rate-limit current_price/change_24h silently stay stale (no error surfaced to client).

action_items: Add a fallback price provider (or use existing price_service.py / price_cache.py) when CoinGecko returns 429. Free-tier CoinGecko throttles aggressively in the preview env and will likely affect production trial users.
Get-alerts list endpoint should not silently drop price refresh failures; either return last_known_price with a stale-flag or log a warning per asset.
Stripe checkout flow (create-checkout) is implemented but not exercised end-to-end; recommend a smoke test once STRIPE_API_KEY is set in env.

SendGrid + Twilio notification keys are not configured (per task note); notification dispatch is therefore skipped silently. Consider surfacing a banner in the dashboard if user picked 'sms' or 'both' but SMS isn't configured server-side.
critical_code_review_comments: alert_routes.py is 711 lines and mixes route handlers, subscription/trial logic, Stripe checkout, Stripe webhooks, and asset search - split into alert_crud.py + alert_subscription.py + alert_webhooks.py for maintainability.

alert_routes.get_alerts iterates alerts and awaits alert_service.get_price sequentially -> N HTTP calls per request, exacerbating CoinGecko 429s. Batch via /simple/price?ids=a,b,c (single request) or asyncio.gather with a limited semaphore.
STRIPE_ALERT_PRICE_ID / PRODUCT_ID are hard-coded in models/alert_models.py - move to env vars to avoid leaking IDs in git and to allow per-env overrides.
Webhook signature verification is optional (falls back to unsigned JSON if STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET unset). Acceptable for dev but must be enforced in production.

AlertService.crypto_id_map is a static dict; unknown symbols fall back to symbol.lower() which yields 404 from CoinGecko silently. Consider an /api/coins/list lookup at startup or warn user during alert creation.

test_credentials.note: /app/memory/test_credentials.md does NOT exist - flag for main agent to add.

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted one TEST_alert_pytest ETH price_above alert during pytest CRUD flow. Pre-existing BTC alert ($100,000 price_above) is left untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Price Alerts pivot is verified working end-to-end against mobiletest@test.com (trial 6 days remaining, 1 BTC alert in DB). Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_alert_routes.py is the regression suite for /api/alerts/*. Known external dependency: CoinGecko free tier (no API key) is rate-limited - flaky price endpoint tests are expected unless backend adds caching/fallback. Stripe + SendGrid + Twilio keys are unset, so checkout and notification dispatch were not exercised.
